title: brassica nigra subsp. hispida
intro: Brassica nigra subsp. hispida, also known as black mustard, is a plant species within the Brassicaceae family, characterized by its dark green leaves and small, yellow flowers. Traditionally, black mustard has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of black mustard include its potential to aid in digestion, relieve respiratory issues, and provide antioxidant properties. Black mustard can be prepared as an herbal remedy through infusions, decoctions, and topical applications, as well as being used as a spice in culinary dishes.
